question_text: A student observes that when a wave enters a new medium, both its speed and wavelength change, but something remains constant. Explain which property of the wave must remain unchanged and why.
correct_answer: Frequency remains constant. The frequency depends only on the source of the wave, not on the medium it travels through. When a wave changes medium, the speed changes due to the different properties of the medium, and wavelength adjusts accordingly (λ = v/f) to keep frequency constant.
explanation: Frequency is determined by what creates the wave (the source), so it doesn't change when entering a new medium. The speed changes because different materials affect how fast waves travel. Since v = f × λ, if v changes and f stays the same, then λ must change too.
